content.text:
<user_query>
Verify this issue exists and fix it: The "Add Apt, suite, etc." button hover color was migrated from `hover:text-neutral-700` (a slightly darker neutral) to `hover:text-primary-700` (a brand color). This silently changes a neutral text affordance to a saturated brand-colored hover, which is inconsistent with how other neutral hover states were migrated elsewhere in the same diff (most became `hover:text-foreground` or kept a muted hover). The base text token `text-muted-foreground` is correct, but the hover token appears mismapped. ```typescriptreact
</user_query>

Searching the codebase for the "Add Apt, suite" button and its hover styles.

[REDACTED]

Checking how similar muted-text buttons use hover in these files and the design system.

[REDACTED]

Confirming the issue: three files use `text-muted-foreground` with `hover:text-primary-700` on the "Add Apt, suite, etc." button. Elsewhere the repo uses `text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground` for neutral icon/text affordances. Replacing `hover:text-primary-700` with `hover:text-foreground` in all three files.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string: className="flex gap-1 items-center self-start mb-1 w-auto text-xs text-muted-foreground hover:text-primary-700"
input.new_string: className="flex gap-1 items-center self-start mb-1 w-auto text-xs text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground"

input.old_string: className="flex gap-1 items-center self-start mb-1 w-auto text-xs text-muted-foreground hover:text-primary-700"
input.new_string: className="flex gap-1 items-center self-start mb-1 w-auto text-xs text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground"

input.old_string: className="flex gap-1 items-center self-start mb-1 w-auto text-xs text-muted-foreground hover:text-primary-700"
input.new_string: className="flex gap-1 items-center self-start mb-1 w-auto text-xs text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground"

content.text:
### Verification

The issue was real. The **Add Apt, suite, etc.** control is a neutral-looking inline button (`text-muted-foreground`) but used **`hover:text-primary-700`** in three places:

- `apps/donor/src/components/address-fields.tsx`
- `apps/donor/src/components/donation-address-fields.tsx`
- `apps/org/src/components/settings/organization-info-page/accept-ownership.tsx`

That turns hover into a brand color instead of a stronger neutral, unlike patterns such as `packages/ui/src/components/file-upload.tsx` and `packages/ui/src/components/tags/tag-filter.tsx`, which use **`text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground`**.

### Fix

Hover was updated from **`hover:text-primary-700`** to **`hover:text-foreground`** in all three files so the affordance stays neutral: muted by default, standard foreground on hover.

[REDACTED]
